description: Native app surfaces phase A3b (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): the default-device path. On a capable host (Linux + KVM with a running or bootable emulator) Doc Detective resolves the default Android device, opens the Settings app through the lazily-installed UiAutomator2 driver, captures it, and closes the surface. On every incapable host (Windows/macOS runners, or Linux without KVM) the android preflight lands the context SKIPPED (no hardware acceleration) — asserted gating, not a gap. No `device` is specified anywhere, so this exercises default-device resolution (reuse a running emulator, else the doc-detective AVD, else create it).

tests.description: Open the preinstalled Settings app (present on every Android system image), wait for it to become ready, screenshot it, and close the surface. Element-level find/click are exercised in the multi-app spec; this one keeps to boot/session/capture/teardown so it is robust across API levels.
